prompt: Asset type: reader-facing academic systems architecture diagram. Question: How do Claude Code responsibilities depend across five semantic layers? Create a clean five-band horizontal architecture diagram on a warm white background, inspired by restrained research-paper infographics but not copying any source figure. Each band spans the page and appears exactly once, top to bottom. Band 1 header SURFACE, containing exactly CLI / REPL and Headless / SDK. Band 2 header CORE, containing exactly Shared Query Loop and Compaction Pipeline. Band 3 header SAFETY / ACTION, containing exactly Capabilities, Permission + Hooks, and Subagents. Band 4 header STATE, containing exactly Context Assembly, Transcript JSONL, and CLAUDE.md + Memory. Band 5 header BACKEND, containing exactly Sandbox / Shell, Workspace, and External Services. Use a few clear dependency arrows: both surface components into Shared Query Loop; Context Assembly into Shared Query Loop; Transcript JSONL two-way with Shared Query Loop; Shared Query Loop into Capabilities; Capabilities into Permission + Hooks; Permission + Hooks into Sandbox / Shell; Subagents connect to Shared Query Loop and Workspace; Sandbox / Shell into Workspace and External Services. Use flat semantic colors: teal surface, charcoal core, amber safety/action, gray-blue state, coral backend. Strong hierarchy, generous whitespace, readable labels, small simple icons, 4-8px corners. Exact visible text only from listed labels and band headers. Every label exactly once. No gradients, no 3D, no decorative prose, no watermark, no logos, no directory tree, no claim of physical process isolation, no Runtime Verified label.

Asset type: reader-facing academic semantic injection map. Question: Where do Claude Code's MCP Servers, Plugins, Skills, and Hooks enter the agent loop, and what context cost do they create? Warm white background. Draw one central left-to-right loop with exactly three large checkpoints in order: ASSEMBLE, MODEL SURFACE, AUTHORIZE / EXECUTE. Under ASSEMBLE put the small subtitle what the model sees. Under MODEL SURFACE put what it can call. Under AUTHORIZE / EXECUTE put whether and how it runs. Around the loop place each extension label exactly once: Skills above ASSEMBLE with an arrow only to ASSEMBLE and badge LOW INITIAL CONTEXT; MCP Servers above MODEL SURFACE with an arrow only to MODEL SURFACE and badge SCHEMA COST; Hooks below with one arrow to ASSEMBLE and another arrow to AUTHORIZE / EXECUTE and badge EVENT DRIVEN; Plugins on the far left as a packaging bracket that fans out to Skills, MCP Servers, and Hooks. Do not connect Plugins directly to a loop checkpoint. Use flat colors: blue loop checkpoints, green capability, amber lifecycle/governance, purple-gray packaging, dark charcoal text. Thick unambiguous arrows, no crossings, generous whitespace, short readable labels, restrained research-paper infographic style. Exact visible text only from the labels and subtitles listed. Every component label exactly once. No gradients, no 3D, no watermark, no logos, no direct Skills to execution arrow, no Hooks to MODEL SURFACE arrow, no claim that Plugins are an execution backend.

Asset type: reader-facing evidence ladder infographic. Question: How do first-party product stances map through analyst synthesis to source mechanisms in Claude Code? Warm white background. Make exactly three vertical columns with headers PRODUCT STANCE (D), ANALYST PRINCIPLE (I), SOURCE MECHANISM (S). Use exactly five aligned rows. Row 1: Human Authority -> Deny-first + Graduated Trust -> Rules + Re-approval. Row 2: Safety + Privacy -> Defense in Depth -> Permission + Sandbox. Row 3: Reliable Execution -> Progressive Context -> Five-stage Compaction. Row 4: Capability Amplification -> Composable Extensions -> MCP + Plugins + Skills + Hooks. Row 5: Context Adaptability -> Append-only + Isolated Work -> JSONL + Sidechains + Worktrees. Put a small ANALYST badge inside every middle-column box. Use dotted arrows from left to middle to show interpretation and solid arrows from middle to right to show source mapping. At the bottom add exactly: D = first-party stance   I = analyst synthesis   S = source mechanism. Flat academic palette with teal left, amber middle, charcoal/blue right; thin row guides, generous whitespace, readable text, 4-8px corners. Exact visible text only from all labels listed. Every row label exactly once. No gradients, no 3D, no watermark, no logos, no statement that the five values or principles are Anthropic's official taxonomy, no causal certainty language.
